title: eval.json — seed-eval target manifest
description: One manifest per eval target (evals/<name>/eval.json). Declares the environment runner, how to materialize+build source/, the project's own tests (optional), the hidden oracle spec, and the Setup-stage inputs (reference captures + the held-out test snapshot). See framework/README.md and the v3 design spec §3.

name.description: Target name; must equal the evals/<name>/ directory.

environment.description: Selects the environment runner (§8). docker = agent+software co-located in a container; macos-vm = agent on host driving a headless guest over SSH.

image.description: The environment image. TWO MODES (docker): (a) BUILD-LOCAL — a logical tag built from the eval's own Dockerfile at evals/<target>/images/<image>/Dockerfile (the default; used when that file exists). (b) EXPLICIT-EXTERNAL — a registry/tag/digest-qualified ref (e.g. node:20.18.1-bookworm-slim) pulled at Setup; this REQUIRES `pull: true` so an arbitrary/typo'd manifest string is never silently run. For macos-vm this is the VM golden-image name.

pull.description: OPTIONAL (docker). Set true to EXPLICITLY opt into pulling `image` as an external registry ref when no local Dockerfile is present. Setup FAILS CLOSED otherwise: no Dockerfile + no `pull:true` => error, never a silent pull. Use a pinned tag or digest.

source.description: OPTIONAL. How Setup materializes source/. When present, Setup clones repo@sha into source/ and asserts HEAD==sha. When absent, source/ is expected to be pre-populated (a committed full project that has no upstream repo to clone).

ref.description: Human-readable tag/branch for provenance (informational; sha is authoritative).

build.description: OPTIONAL. Build commands Setup runs in the environment to stand up the original. Required in practice for any target Setup must build (the docker lane errors clearly if absent).

tests.description: OPTIONAL. The project's OWN test command, run from the scorer-only held-out copy (Setup snapshot). Present only where the project has tests (e.g. `npx vitest run`; omitted for projects without a test suite).

scoring.description: OPTIONAL per-project SCORING RECIPE. The Evaluator rolls the listed component signals into ONE composite TREND number per run (weighted average over the PRESENT components); every component is ALSO shown in the scorecard breakdown. This is a trend signal (is seed-create holding/improving), NOT pass/fail — there are no gates. A CLI target might be tests-dominated; a GUI target might be behavioral-checks + screenshot-match (no tests). When absent, the Evaluator falls back to a built-in default recipe.

components.description: Each component contributes weight*normalizedSignal to the composite. A component whose signal cannot be measured (e.g. a test-harness failure) is EXCLUDED and the remaining weights renormalize — a harness failure never tanks the trend number; it is surfaced as its own visible component.

key.description: stable component id (e.g. projectTests, ourCriteria, visual, build, codeCopy, behavioralChecks, screenshotMatch).

metric.description: ratio = num/den from evidence; value = a 0..1 field; build = buildOk?1:0; boolPenalty = field truthy→0 else 1 (penalize, never zero the whole number).

evidence.description: evidence filename under score/evidence/ (e.g. tests.json, criteria.json, visual.json, code-copy.json, or a lane-specific file).

harnessGuard.description: If set, the component is treated as ABSENT (excluded + surfaced as a harness failure) when its evidence status indicates the harness could not run (a setup/build failure), rather than counting as a 0 fidelity result.

oracle.description: The hidden evaluation material (§2). Paths are relative to the eval dir. Never materialized into the Creator or Installer workspace.

reference.description: Path to the captured-reference dir (the original's evidence).

setup.description: Inputs the Setup stage needs: how to capture reference evidence, and where/what to snapshot as the held-out test copy.

referenceCaptures.description: Canonical invocations of the built original whose output becomes oracle/reference/<id>. For docker/CLI targets, argv is passed to the project's CLI bin; stdout is saved.

testsLocked.description: Path (under the eval dir, inside oracle/) where Setup snapshots the held-out copy of the project's tests. Scorer-only.

testGlobs.description: Globs (relative to source/) of the files to snapshot as the held-out test copy. Required when `tests` is present.

lockfile.description: Lockfile to include in the snapshot for deterministic scorer installs (optional).

expectedTestCount.description: Sanity anchor: the green test count expected at the pin (Setup asserts the discovered total matches).
